query: What is the capacity threshold that triggers the large generation interconnection process in NYISO according to the NYISO process manual 23 and guide UG21? Cite the relevant source report and section you got this from
final_answer:
## Capacity Threshold for the Large Generation Interconnection Process in NYISO

The capacity threshold that triggers the **Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP)** — NYISO's large generation interconnection process — is **20 MW**.

### Key Details

Generating facilities with a total output **exceeding 20 MW** are subject to the Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP), while facilities at or below 20 MW fall under the **Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP)**. This 20 MW dividing line is established in the NYISO OATT and reflected in both Manual 23 and Guide UG-21.

### Sources and Sections

1. **NYISO OATT Attachment X (Section 30)** — The title of Attachment X itself states it is the *"Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (Applicable to Generating Facilities that exceed 20 MWs and to Class Year Transmission Facilities)"*. Additionally, **Section 30.3.1** explicitly states: *"The requirement to submit an Interconnection Request applies to all Large Facilities seeking evaluation under this Attachment X to the ISO OATT, including (1) material modifications; (2) increases in capacity that results in total output in excess of 20 MW."*
   - Source: [NYISO OATT Section 30 (Attachment X)](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/33257202/OATT%20Section%2030%20-%20Full.pdf/ee515fbd-ef9e-0201-014e-beaa42a2c353) and [OATT Section 30.3.1](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%20Section%2030_HSR%20Revisions.pdf/9a47d1ba-7ee1-6789-f6b2-04acb82afa7d)

2. **NYISO Manual 23 — Transmission Expansion and Interconnection Manual** (Version 4.3, Effective Date 02/07/2025) — **Section 3.3** covers the "Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP)" and **Section 3.4** covers the "Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP)." The manual references OATT Attachment X (LFIP) and Attachment Z (SGIP) as the governing tariff provisions, which establish the 20 MW threshold.
   - Source: [NYISO Manual 23, Version 4.3](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/2924447/M-23-TEI-v4.2-Memo-Final.pdf/94a26e65-fd68-98e1-535b-fc41a9536607)

3. **NYISO UG-21 — Transmission Expansion and Interconnection User's Guide** (Version 3.0, Effective Date 07/18/2025) — **Section 6** ("What is the study process for generation interconnection projects?") describes the interconnection processes for generating facilities and references the applicable tariff provisions including OATT Attachment HH (the new Standard Interconnection Procedures) and the TEI Manual. The guide notes that the NYISO's interconnection process evaluates proposed interconnections of Generating Facilities and that jurisdiction (including the size-based process selection) is a threshold issue.
   - Source: [NYISO UG-21, Version 3.0](https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v1.0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28)

### Important Caveat on Recent Changes

As noted in Manual 23 (Version 4.3), on May 1, 2024, NYISO filed extensive revisions (FERC Docket No. ER24-1915) in response to FERC Order No. 2023, establishing new **Standard Interconnection Procedures (SIP)** in **Attachment HH** of the OATT. These new SIP procedures replace the former LFIP (Attachments S, X) and SGIP (Attachment Z). Under the new SIP, all generating facilities intending to participate in NYISO wholesale sales are processed through a unified cluster study process, regardless of size. However, the **20 MW threshold** remains the historical dividing line under the prior LFIP/SGIP framework that Manual 23 Section 3.3 and the associated tariff attachments established.

steps.reasoning:
Based on my research, I now have a clear picture. The key finding is from the NYISO OATT Attachment X (Section 30), which is referenced by both Manual 23 and UG-21. The threshold is **20 MW**.

Specifically:

1. **NYISO OATT Attachment X** (Section 30) states: "Attachment X – Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (Applicable to Generating Facilities that exceed 20 MWs and to Class Year Transmission Facilities)"

2. **NYISO OATT Attachment X, Section 30.3.1** states: "The requirement to submit an Interconnection Request applies to all Large Facilities seeking evaluation under this Attachment X to the ISO OATT, including (1) material modifications; (2) increases in capacity that results in total output in excess of 20 MW"

3. **NYISO Manual 23** (Transmission Expansion and Interconnection Manual, Version 4.3, February 2025) - Section 3.3 covers the "Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP)" and Section 3.4 covers "Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP)". The manual references Attachment X for the LFIP and Attachment Z for the SGIP.

4. **NYISO UG-21** (Transmission Expansion and Interconnection User's Guide, Version 3.0, July 2025) - Section 6 describes the study process for generation interconnection projects.

The threshold is **20 MW**. Generating facilities that exceed 20 MW are subject to the Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP), while those at or below 20 MW fall under the Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP).

Note: With the new Standard Interconnection Procedures (SIP) under Attachment HH (filed May 2024 in response to FERC Order No. 2023), the old LFIP/SGIP distinction is being replaced. However, the 20 MW threshold was the dividing line under the previous procedures referenced in Manual 23 and UG-21.

Let me also note that the UG-21 guide now references the new Standard Interconnection Procedures (SIP) under Attachment HH, which applies to "Generating Facilities that intend to participate in the NYISO wholesale sales" without specifying a MW threshold - the new SIP appears to apply to all generating facilities regardless of size. But the question specifically asks about the threshold that triggers the "large generation interconnection process," which under the historical/pre-Order 2023 framework was 20 MW.
